name: Flor de Amaras
instructions:
*Hibiscus Simple Syrup:
1:1 w/ a cup of dried hibiscus steeping for 30-40 min

name: The Happy Place
instructions:
*House-made Cranberry syrup: 
-- 2 cups Fresh Cranberries
-- 1 cup Sugar
-- 1 cup Water
-- 2 Bay Leaves
-- .25 cup Pink Peppercorns
-- Half Serrano Chile
-- 4 Sprigs Fresh Rosemary

Add all ingredients to a pot and heat thoroughly. Simmer on low until cranberries cook down for 25 minutes. Strain and let cool.

name: Bon Voyage Pisco Punch
instructions:
*Pineapple Gomme: 
Mix equal parts (1.5 cups) gum arabic with water over high heat until it all mixes and then let cool for a bit. Then you're gonna make a sugar syrup with 2 parts sugar, 1 part water (4 cups water, 2 cups white granulated sugar) in the same manner over high heat until it mixes, and then add the gum syrup to the mix until everything dissolves and what you're left with is a thick gummy syrup that resembles a whole lot of baby batter. Then cut up 1.5 cups of pineapple chunks, put in mason jar, and pour the gum syrup over it and let it sit in the refrigerator over night. A few hours would work also if you don't have that much time. However, the more time, the more pineapple infusion takes place. When it's ready, strain into another mason jar or bottle, and it's ready to go.

name: Still Life of a Pineapple
instructions: *Pineapple Syrup: Equal parts pineapple blended with water and sugar and strained

name: The Bittered Valley
instructions:
1st glass ingredients:
Luxardo Maraschino, Acid Phosphate

2nd glass ingredients:
BarSol Quebranta Pisco, Luxardo Amaro Abano, Luxardo Fernet, Scrappy's Aromatic Bitters

name: OH Snap!
instructions:
Add 6-8 sugar snap peas to cocktail shaker. Lightly muddle the peas. Add all other ingredients except for the tonic. Shake and double strain into a collins glass. Top with tonic. 

*House-made Simple Syrup: 
-- 1 cup Sugar
-- 1 cup Water

Add equal parts sugar and water into a small saucepan, bring to a boil; simmer until the sugar is dissolved, approximately 3 minutes. Remove from heat, let cool to room temperature and transfer to a clean glass jar. Cover and keep refrigerated for up to 2 weeks.

name: Wabash Avenue Sour
instructions: Add all ingredients and dry-shake (without ice), then add ice, and shake again. Strain into coupe and garnish with rhubarb bitters.

name: IPA Mule
instructions: Add all ingredients except beer into shaker tin, shake vigorously for 10 seconds. Add beer in shaker tin then double strain into tin cup. Add mint for garish.

name: Ritz Cocktail
instructions: Add all ingredients except the champagne to a mixing glass and fill with ice. Stir, and strain into a martini glass. Fill with champagne and garnish with a flamed orange peel.

name: King's Snap the Chocolate Out of You
instructions: Add all ingredients except the whip cream and ginger snap. Shake and double into a rocks glass with no ice. Top with marshmallow whip cream. Garnish with Ginger snap crumbles.

name: Happiness
instructions:
Add all ingredients in a cocktail shaker, shake, fine strain into a chilled coupe or martini glass. Garnish with edible flower.

*Jasmine Tea/Rice-Infused Junipero Gin:
-- 1 bottle (750ml) Junípero gin
-- .5 cup Jasmine Tea
-- .5 cup Jasmine Rice (wash rice to eliminate starch)

Sous Vide Version: Wash the jasmine rice to clean out the starch. Fill container with hot water. Set sous vide at 52c. Add vacuum seal bag with jasmine tea/rice in bath. Let sit in bath for 45 minutes. Take the bag out of bath and sit it in ice bath for 15 minutes. Open bag and fine strain back into bottle.

Infusion Version: Wash the jasmine rice to clean out the starch. In a cambro/mason jar, add Junípero gin and jasmine tea and rice. Let infuse for 24 hours. Fine strain into bottle.

**House-made Honey Syrup: 

Put equal parts of honey (10 oz) and water (10 oz) in a saucepan. Boil, then turn down to a simmer and stir until the honey is completely dissolved.

name: Fourth Generation
instructions: Add all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir with ice. Strain into a Nick & Nora coupe. Garnish with a ghost orange twist.

name: West Coast Punch
instructions: Add all ingredients in a mixing glass with fresh ice. Shake vigorously and strain into a Nick & Nora glass. Garnish with lime pinwheel and bing cherry.

name: Mille Miglia
instructions: Add all ingredients in a mixing glass with fresh ice. Shake vigorously and strain into a Nick & Nora glass. Garnish with lime pinwheel and bing cherry.

name: Paradiso Sun
instructions: Add all ingredients in a mixing glass with fresh ice. Shake vigorously and strain into a Nick & Nora glass. Garnish with orange slice and 1 bar spoon juniper berries.

name: Lux Orchard Lemonade
instructions: Add all ingredients in a mixing glass with fresh ice. Shake vigorously and strain over fresh ice. Garnish with Luxardo Cherry.

name: Cut & Rum
instructions: Add all ingredients in mixing glass, stir and strain over 2x2 ice cube in smoked glass on hickory board. Garnish with orange peel and clothes pin.

name: The King's Spring Daisy
instructions: Add all ingredients into a cocktail shaker. Shake and double strain into a glass. Garnish with a sprig of mint.

name: The King's Ginger Spiced Punch
instructions: Add all ingredients into a punch bowl or pitcher and stir. Add lemon wheels and strawberries to garnish.

name: War of the Roses
instructions: Add all ingredients into a punch bowl. Serve in small rocks glasses.

name: The King's Medicine
instructions: Add all ingredients into a shaker with ice, shake furiously. Fine strain into chilled coupe. Garnish with a lemon twist.

name: A.Nise
instructions: Add all ingredients into a shaker with ice. Shake. Strain over fresh ice into a rocks glass. Garnish with star anise and two slices lemongrass.

name: Down the Rabbit Hole
instructions: Add all ingredients into a shaker, shake strain into a collins glass, fill with crushed ice, garnish with mint leaves.

name: Peace Daiquiri
instructions: Add all ingredients into shaking tin. Add ice cubes to tin. Shake vigorously and strain into coupe. Garnish with Luxardo Maraschino Cherry.

name: The King's Autumn Cobbler
instructions: Add all ingredients over ice. Stir with a barspoon. Squeeze lemon wedges and add blackberries.

name: The Mad King
instructions: Add all ingredients to a mixing glass and stir for 15-20 seconds. Strain into a highball filled with fresh ice.

name: The Robert Baratheon
instructions: Add all ingredients to a mixing glass filled with ice. Stir until cold and strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Lux Shandy
instructions: Add all ingredients to a mixing glass. Add ice and integration shake. Pour over fresh ice into a highball and top with California Lager. Garnish with a half orange wheel.

name: Fiona Graham
instructions: Add all ingredients to a mixing glass. Stir until thoroughly chilled (~30 seconds). Strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Serve with one big rock. Garnish with an expressed orange swath.

name: Le Sang et Sable
instructions: Add all ingredients to a shaker and fill with ice. Shake vigorously and strain into chilled stemmed cordial glass. Garnish with cocktail cherry and lemon twist.

name: Martinez
instructions: Add all ingredients to a shaker and fill with ice. Shake, and strain into a chilled coupe glass.

name: Amaretto Sour BV-Style
instructions: Add all ingredients to a shaker and shake well without ice to emulsify the egg white. Add ice and shake hard until the drink is chilled. Strain through a fine-mesh strainer into a chilled coupe and garnish.

name: Julia's Child
instructions: Add all ingredients to a shaker and shake with ice, then double strain into a cocktail glass. Garnish with a celery leaf.

name: The Bitter King
instructions: Add all ingredients to a shaker, add ice, shake and strain into a martini glass garnish with a Luxardo Cherry.

name: La Maracuja
instructions: Add all ingredients to blender and blend throughly. Double strain into a glass with cracked ice garnish with a mint sprig and fresh grated nutmeg.

name: San Francisco Strong (Skrong)
instructions:
Add all ingredients to tin and dry shake. Add ice and vigorously shake for 12 seconds. Fine strain into chilled cocktail glass. Garnish by sprinkling matcha powder (.25 tsp). 

*Rich Simple Syrup: 
-- 2 cups Granulated Sugar
-- 1 cup Water

Combine sugar and water in a small saucepan over medium heat and stir until sugar dissolves. Remove from heat, let cool to room temperature and transfer to a clean glass jar. Cover and keep refrigerated for up to 2 weeks.

name: Nuestra Julep
instructions: Add all ingredients together.

name: Pisco Punch
instructions: Add all liquid ingredients into a cocktail shaker with ice. Shake well and strain into an ice filled white wine glass. Garnish by adding pineapple chunks and Luxardo Cherry.

name: La Batida Loca
instructions:
Add all of the ingredients, except seeds, to a shaker with ice and shake well. Double strain onto fresh ice into a collins glass. Garnish with 1 spoon of passion fruit seeds.

*Pilon Syrup:
Homemade 50/50 simple syrup using Pilon Sugar made from Mexican Sugar Cane.

name: Neighborhood Negroni
instructions: Add all the ingredients to a mixing glass and fill with ice. Stir until well chilled and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a flamed orange twist.

name: Rothes Roy
instructions: Add all the ingredients to a mixing glass and fill with ice. Stir, and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a Luxardo Cherry.

name: Angel's Face
instructions: Add all the ingredients to a mixing glass and fill with ice. Stir, and strain into a cocktail glass. Garnish with a slice of orange peel.

name: Kiss
instructions: Add all the ingredients to a mixing glass and fill with ice. Stir, and strain into a martini glass. Garnish with an apple slice.

name: The Cappa
instructions: Add all the ingredients to a mixing glass and fill with ice. Stir, and strain into a small wine or cocktail glass. Twist a slice of lemon peel over the drink and use as garnish.

name: The Inside Job
instructions: Add all the ingredients to a mixing glass and fill with ice. Stir, and strain into an old fashioned glass filled with fresh ice. Garnish with a Luxardo Cherry and an orange slice or twist.

name: The Sun Also Rises
instructions: Add all the ingredients to a shaker and fill with ice. Shake, and strain into a chilled coupe glass filled with crushed ice.

name: Trato Hecho
instructions:
Add all the ingredients to a shaker and fill with ice. Shake, and strain into a coupe glass.

*Pineapple-Infused Mezcal:
Peel, core and chop a whole pineapple into 1-inch cubes. Add to a large jar or other container with a lid, and add a 750-mL bottle of mezcal. Let stand for 5 days and strain before using.

name: Nick & Janae
instructions:
Add cognac, amaro, lemon, strawberry syrup and fresh cracked egg white of a whole egg to tin. Dry shake without ice approx. 5 seconds to emulsify. Add ice and shake hard again approx. 10 seconds. Fine strain into coupe. Crack drag black pepper over surface. Serve and enjoy.

*Strawberry syrup- 1 cup fresh strawberries 1 cup water 1cup sugar. Blend in vitamix then heat on stove and stir until hot to bind sugars, don't boil. Bottle and refrigerate.

name: On the Wings of Eagles
instructions: Add cognac, rye, Chartreuse and bitters to a mixing glass with ice, and stir until cold. Add splash of fernet to a rocks glass, roll the glass to coat the sides, and discard the excess. Add one large ice cube to the glass, strain the drink over the cube, and garnish with a mint sprig.

name: Mule Pigeon
instructions: Add cubed ice to a highball, add 2 dashes of Angostura Bitters, top with ginger beer, garnish with a sprig of basil.

name: Cuba Liberation
instructions: Add first three ingredients to a mixing glass with shaker and lightly shake with ice. Pour mixture into a Collins or highball glass over ice. Top with Mexican Coke (or any coca cola as a substitute) and pour Luxardo Fernet on top to float. Garnish with a lime wheel and fresh mint sprig.

name: Off the Radar
instructions: Add gin, lemon juice, maraschino liqueur, and liqueur de violette to a collins glass filled with ice. Add tonic water; stir to combine. Garnish with a lemon wheel.

name: Maraschino Side Car
instructions: Add ingredients in a mixing glass with fresh ice. Shake vigorously strain over fresh ice. Garnish with lemon peel to fit in glass and 1 dash of Abbott's Aromatic Bitters. Optional sugar rim.

name: The Royal Buck
instructions: Add ingredients to a mixing glass except club soda. Shake over ice, strain over ice into a collins glass, top with club soda. Garnish with a lemon wedge and candied ginger on a pick.

name: Rx Hot Toddy
instructions:
Add ingredients to a mug and stir.

*Honey-Ginger Syrup:
Combine 1 part ginger with 2 parts honey and 1 cup of water and boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 5 mins. Strain and discard solids.

**Ghost-Pepper-Infused Campari:
Soak 1 ghost pepper in a 16 oz. bottle of Campari for up to 24 hrs (reduce infusion time to reduce heat).

***Cinnamon Tincture:
Soak 3 cinnamon sticks in 16 oz. of any neutral grain alcohol that is high proof (regular 80-proof vodka is great) for up to 14 days. Strain and discard the sticks.

name: Spanish G&T
instructions:
Add Junipero Gin and botanicals first. Add ice and follow with a bold tonic. Top with additional botanicals/garnish for presentation.

*Assorted Complementing Botanicals: Orange, Star Anise, Rose Petals, Italian Juniper

name: Chilcano
instructions: Add pisco and lime juice into a highball glass filled with ice. Pour ginger ale into the glass to top. Add 2-3 drops of bitters. Garnish with lime wheel.

name: Kir Royale
instructions: Add room temperature Creme de Cassis to the glass first, followed by the Champagne.

name: Kir
instructions: Add room temperature Creme de Cassis to the glass first, followed by the wine.

name: Citrus Agave Caipirinha
instructions: Add soda to bucket first, then add spirits and mixers. Top with ice and garnish.

name: Bermuda Highball
instructions: Add spirits and bitters to collins glass. Add large ice cubes and fill with club soda. Garnish with orange peel (or lemon peel).

name: Lux Cherry Sparkler
instructions: Add the Cherry Liqueur to a flute, top with Prosecco, and garnish with a Luxardo Maraschino Cherry.

name: French Maid
instructions: Add the cucumber, mint and simple syrup to a mixing glass and muddle. Add everything else, then shake with ice and strain into a Collins glass filled with ice. Garnish with a mint sprig poking through a cucumber wheel.

name: Karlsson's Mule
instructions: Add vodka and lime juice to drinking glass, add ice, and top with ginger beer. Garnish with a lime.

name: Creek Ran Dry
instructions:
Barrel aged for 5 months in a used bourbon barrel.

Stir with ice and strain into a small coupe. Garnish with an orange twist.

name: Mandrake
instructions:
Barrel aged in a used bourbon barrel that previously held a cocktail containing Hine Cognac and Luxardo Bitter.

Stir with ice and strain into a small coupe. Garnish with an orange twist.

name: Redondo Beach
instructions: Blend with ice.

name: The Tahoe Toddy
instructions:
Boil water in a tea kettle. 

Squeeze lemon slice into the mug. Insert whole cloves into the lemon slice and drop it into the mug.

name: Pink Ribbon
instructions: Brown sugar cube soaked in Peychaud’s Bitters. Put soaked sugar cube in glass first. Pour .75 oz Pink Pigeon rum into flute over sugar cube. Fill remainder of the glass with champagne. Garnish with lemon twist.

name: Short Round II
instructions: Build & Stir. Hoshizaki Ice.

name: A Gift from Zara
instructions: Build all ingredients except for the Topo Chico in a wine glass. Fill the glass with cubed ice then top with Topo Chico by pouring it down a barspoon to incorporate. Zest the top with orange and place it in the glass. Add edible flowers for aroma and aesthetic.

name: Gin Made Me Do It
instructions: Build all ingredients in a mixing glass, add ice and stir. Strain into a chilled martini glass and garnish with fresh fennel flowers.

name: Clearly Bitter
instructions: Build all ingredients in a mixing glass. Stir with ice and strain into coupe. Garnish with grapefruit peel.

name: Short Round
instructions: Build all ingredients in a punch bowl and add ice, stir gently. Garnish with seasonal fruit and citrus wheels.

name: Ypioca Yellow Card
instructions: Build all into highball. Fill with ice. Top with soda water. Place pineapple leaf vertically in the glass.

name: Buzzard's Bay
instructions: Build and pour in punch bowl over large ice block.

name: Kick the Pigeon
instructions: Build and serve in a champagne flute.

name: Croatia Firing Squad
instructions: Build cocktail in glass, add ice and stir. Garnish with rose petal.

name: The King's Ginger Summer Cup
instructions: Build in a glass over ice. Add lemon wheels and strawberries. Finish with a splash of fresh lemon or top up with some sparkling wine. This drink can also be made in jugs or punch bowls.

name: Pink Pigeon Punch
instructions: Build in a large pitcher or punchbowl. Combine Pink Pigeon Rum, juice from 2 lemons and agave. Blend 7 oz. of fresh watermelon in a blender and puree to mixture. Add a generous amount of ice and stir. Top with ginger beer. Ladle or pour into coupe glasses.

name: Kentucky Garrigue
instructions: Build in a mixing glass and add ice. Slowly pour back and forth between two tins, about 4-6 times. Then dump the whole cocktail into a Fizz or Collins glass.

name: Bitter Valencia
instructions:
Build in a mixing glass. Add ice. Stir to dilute. Strain into a Nick & Nora glass. Garnish with an orange twist.

*Clarified Milk:
-- 1 C Whole Milk
-- .5 oz Lemon Juice

Heat milk to just before a simmer, then add lemon juice. Lightly stir to break the solid from the whey. Let chill for 5 mins to allow the solids to properly separate. Coffee strain twice for clarity.

name: Chilcano (variation)
instructions: Build in an ice filled 14 oz beer glass, add BarSol Pisco, Ginger Liqueur, fresh lime juice, and agave nectar. Roll into a Boston shaker and shake to mix, place cranberry bitters on top, add ginger beer and garnish with a wedge of fresh lime.

name: Little Ground Tapper
instructions: Build in an old fashioned glass over ice. Garnish with orange peel.

name: Prince of Humbugs
instructions: Build in collins glass. Top with soda. Orange peel.

name: The Big Picture
instructions: Build in glass. Add crushed ice and swizzle. Top with more crushed ice. Dash pepita tincture on top for aromatics. Garnish with lemon wheel and dried fig.

name: The King's Squeeze
instructions: Build in ice-filled tall glass, garnish with a squeeze of lime.

name: English Harbour Colada
instructions: Build in old fashioned glass over ice.

name: Rita's Song
instructions: Build in old fashioned glass, stir and serve over large ice cube.

name: Le Femme Nikita
instructions: Build in rocks glass. Use cylinder ice cube.

name: 1880's Americana
instructions: Build over ice in Old Fashioned glass.

name: Luxardo Maraschino Simple Spritz
instructions: Build over ice, stir lightly, and garnish with a lime twist and a Luxardo Cherry.

name: Aperitivo Spritz
instructions: Build over ice, stir lightly, and garnish with an orange peel or slice and Luxardo Cherry.

name: La Chiara
instructions: Build spirits in a tall glass over clear ice. Top with 4 oz tonic and garnish with a lemon twist curled into a flower if you're feeling fancy.

name: Luxardo Ice Cream Float
instructions: Build syrup and ice cream in a lowball cocktail glass, and top with soda water. Serve with a metal straw, a spoon, and garnish with a Luxardo Maraschino Cherry.

name: Negroni
instructions: Build the cocktail in a mixing glass. Stir for about 15 seconds and then strain over ice into a old fashioned glass.

name: Nellie Fer' Toddy
instructions: Build then pour into a pre-heated Irish coffee glass or mug. Top with hot water. Garnish with the orange slice and add a dash of Peychaud's Bitters.

name: Almond Crusta
instructions: Chill coupe glass and garnish rim by coating the outer lip of the glass with a wedge of lemon and dipping into a shallow dish containing superfine white sugar. Place lemon strip neatly along side of sugared coupe. Combine all liquid ingredients into shaker; add ice and shake. Double strain into glass.

name: Mexico Mule
instructions: Chill cup and place a napkin around it. In the base of the cup put everything except the cider and the raspberry syrup and give a brief stir. Add some crushed ice. Stir vigorously. Top with crushed ice. Add cider. Pour raspberry syrup on top. Garnish with cinnamon quill as a stirrer, some small flowers, and a lime wheel.

name: Hidarite
instructions: Chilled. Serve up or on the rocks.

name: Bird is the Word
instructions: Cold shake-strain into coupe and garnish with a Luxardo Maraschino Cherry.

name: Jammie Dodger Fizz
instructions: Combine all ingredients and shake thoroughly without ice for several minutes. Add ice, shake, and strain into a tall collins glass. Top with soda and garnish with an orange wheel.

name: Rudolph's Rose
instructions: Combine all ingredients and stir with ice in a mixing glass. Strain into coupe glass and garnish with a Luxardo Cherry.

name: Heard It Through The Grapevine
instructions: Combine all ingredients and stir. Pour the mixture into a chilled Nick & Nora glass and garnish with the orange zest.

name: Snow Bunny
instructions: Combine all ingredients besides beer and bitters and dry shake without ice. Add ice and re-shake, double strain into a fizz glass and top with beer. Garnish with shaved nutmeg and 3 drops of bitters.

name: A Love Song
instructions: Combine all ingredients besides blood orange into a mixing glass and stir until proper dilution (15-20 seconds). Pour into a small martini glass. Rub a thin slice of blood orange around the rim and place in drink.

name: Drops of Jupiter
instructions: Combine all ingredients except Duke's Lemon garnish in mixing glass and stir. Pour into a rocks glass and garnish. Ice does not touch this drink.

name: Mother Superior
instructions:
Combine all ingredients except for tonic. Shake with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe. Top with the tonic and garnish with a fresh grapefruit peel. 

*Substitute with Yellow Chartreuse Liqueur

name: Round Pond Aperitif
instructions: Combine all ingredients except soda in a shaker with ice, shake to combine. Pour into a coupe glass and garnish with a lemon peel.

name: Across The Pond
instructions: Combine all ingredients except soda in a shaker with ice, shake to combine. Pour over ice in a rocks glass and top with soda. Garnish with a Luxardo cherry.

name: Fizzy Rhubarb Mule
instructions:
Combine all ingredients except soda in a tin and dry shake. Add ice and shake vigorously. Add soda into a collins glass and slowly strain the drink over it until it forms a head. Garnish with one rhubarb stalk.

*Rhubarb Infused Gin:
750 ml of gin with 3 chopped stalks of fresh rhubarb for 48 hours

name: Charente Royale
instructions: Combine all ingredients except sparkling wine in a shaker. Add ice and shake until chilled through. Double strain into a champagne flute and garnish with an orange twist.

name: Peachy Keane
instructions: Combine all ingredients except the bitters and dry shake. Add ice and shake again. Strain over fresh ice into a double old fashioned glass. Dash bitters over the top of the cocktail. Garnish with a dried peach and a piece of candied ginger.

name: Hopped-Up Mary
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a chilled mixing glass, rimmed with sea salt and stir. Add ice. Stir again to mix. Garnish with a cocktail shrimp, lemon zest, fresh ground pepper and a celery stalk.

name: The Palace Cellar
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a Collins glass and stir. Garnish with a slice of ginger and lemon wheel.

name: Swedish Royal
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a frozen mixing glass with hard ice and stir for 20 seconds. Strain into a frozen coupe glass.

name: Gold Spritz
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a glass filled with ice and garnish with an orange twist.

name: La Madrugada
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and add ice. Shake well and double strain and serve into a coupe.

name: The Rickenbacker
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir until proper dilution. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a sage leaf speared through two cocktail onions.

name: The Douglas Tilden
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice, stir and strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a lemon twist and the edible flower of your choice.

name: Willow
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass, add ice and stir. Strain into a chilled coupe glass and garnish with a Luxardo Maraschino Cherry.

name: Sahara Glowing Heart
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass, add ice and then stir. Strain into a Nick & Nora glass and garnish with a lemon twist.

name: Nori & Islay
instructions:
Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass, stir, and strain into a chilled Nick & Nora glass. Garnish with a picked cucumber ribbon.

*Nori-Infused Sake: 
-- Ginjo Sake
-- Nori Seaweed Sheet

Using an Oji Style Cold Brew Coffee Maker, drip Ginjo sake through a 0.5 gram of nori seaweed sheet that has been hand-shredded. It takes 3 hours to infuse a 750 ml bottle.

name: You Bet Your Ascot
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass. Stir until 2 oz water is diluted. Strain into a sherry or nosing glass. Flame and discard an orange peel. Drink deeply.

name: Speyside Orchard
instructions:
Combine all ingredients in a mixing tin. Shake and strain into a collins glass full of crushed ice. Garnish with apple slices.

*Chamomile Tea Syrup: 
-- 2 c water
-- 1 c chamomile flower
-- 1 c sugar
Bring 1 cup water to boil.
Add 1 cup chamomile flower and 1 more cup of water, bring back to a boil.
Add 1 cup sugar, bring to boil. 
Let it rest. When cool, the syrup is ready.

name: Chippendale Rosetone
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a punch bowl, add a large chunk or block of ice, garnish with lemon wheels and apple slices. Serve when punch is chilled.

name: La Hops Vie
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker and shake until proper dilution. Double strain into a chilled martini or coupe glass and garnish with 2 fresh basil leaves that have been lightly "spanked" to release aromatics.

name: Cap't of the Seven Seas
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker tin and shake vigorously. Double strain using a fine mesh strainer. Serve in a lager glass over ice.

name: Boko Buku
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker tin and shake vigorously. Double strain with a fine mesh strainer into a highball glass.

name: Chapman Cobbler
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker tin filled with crushed ice. Flash-shake and dump into a double old fashioned glass. Garnish.

name: Baile de Verano
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker tin, add ice, and shake vigorously. Double strain with a fine mesh strainer into a highball glass.

name: Peter Pumpkin
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker, except the apple cider. Shake vigorously. Strain over ice. Top with apple cider. Garnish with shaved cinnamon and nutmeg.

name: A Botanical Garden
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaking tin and give it a short shake, five seconds. Strain into an old fashined glass with three medium sized ice cubes.

name: The Bossa Nova
instructions:
Combine all ingredients in a shaking tin or mixing glass and vigorously shake without ice for 10 seconds. Add ice, and shake for another 8 seconds. Strain over larger ice cube or into a cocktail glass and add freshly grated cinnamon on top.

*Vanilla Syrup: 
Split 1 vanilla pod lengthwise and scrape interior into a heavy sauce pan. 
Add 2 c. raw sugar and 2 c. water. Bring to a simmer and reduce heat to low. 
Cook for 5 mins and stir occasionally. 
Remove from heat and keep unstrained for 24 hrs. 
Strain through a cheese cloth and bottle. Keeps for 2 weeks.

**Coconut Cream:
Combine 1 part Coco Lopez with 1 part rich coconut milk. Mix well.

name: The Monte Rua
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a sparkling wine glass.

name: See ya Amaro!
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a tall glass.

name: Rose Sangria
instructions:
Combine all ingredients in a wine glass, stir to mix. Add ice and enjoy.  
Convert ounces to cups to make a larger pitcher for you and your guests.

name: White Tie
instructions: Combine all ingredients in mixing glass with ice and stir. Strain into chilled cocktail coupe or Nick & Nora. Expel lemon peel over cocktail then place in glass.

name: The Italian Diplomat
instructions:
Combine all ingredients in mixing glass. Stir and strain into Nick & Nora cocktail glass. Garnish with lemon zest and yerba mate tea sachet.

*Bay Leaf Syrup:
-- 16 oz Water
-- 12 Bay Leaves
-- 1.5 C White Sugar

Bring water to boil with bay leaves. Lower heat to medium and simmer 15-20 mins. Stir in white sugar until dissolved, without bringing to a boil again. Remove from heat, let cool at room temperature. Remove bay leaves and enjoy.

**Salt Tincture:
-- .5 C Salt
-- 1 C Water
-- 2 C 100p Vodka

Stir salt with water and vodka. Stir until dissolved. Strain out extra salt, if any.

name: Bunny Poison
instructions:
Combine all ingredients in Perlini sparkling cocktail shaker, shake and double strain into cocktail glass. (For home use, combine all ingredients, add ice shake 30 seconds and double strain into coupe, add splash of soda water.)
Spray with atomized Old Tom Gin.
Garnish with cucumber peel or candied rhubarb slice.

*Rhubarb Cucumber & Mint-Infused Junipero Gin:
-- 1 bottle Junipero Gin
-- 1 qt Chopped Rhubarb
-- 1 Chopped Large English Cucumber
-- .5 Bunch (15g) Fresh Mint (with stems)

Combine all ingredients in vacuum sealed bag and circulate at 53º for 2 hours. (For home use, combine all ingredients in plastic container and let rest for one week.)

*Rhubarb Syrup:
Juice 1 qt (8-10 Stalks)
Add to 1 qt of unbleached Cane sugar in saucepan and bring to simmer.
Remove from heat, allow to cool and strain in Chinoise.

name: See You T'Amaro
instructions: Combine all ingredients in shaker. Dry shake for 10 seconds, add ice and shake for 30 seconds. Strain into small tumbler. Garnish with a sprinkle of grated nutmeg.

name: Cut To The Chase
instructions:
Combine all ingredients in to a mixing glass and add ice.

Stir for about 10-15 seconds.

Strain in to a cocktail coupe or a glass of your choosing.

Garnish with an orange twist expressed over the top of the drink.

name: The Lover's Quarrel
instructions: Combine all ingredients into a large punch bowl and add large ice cube/cubes. Garnish liberally with slice fruits.

name: Hophead Dirty Martini
instructions: Combine all ingredients into a mixing glass and add ice. Shake and strain into a martini glass. Garnish with an olive.

name: Newton's Theory
instructions: Combine all ingredients into a mixing glass and add ice. Stir for about 10 seconds and then strain into a chilled cocktail coupe. Garnish with skewered apple spheres and be thankful you're having a cocktail, and not having an apple shot off your head.

name: West Coast Vesper
instructions: Combine all ingredients into a mixing glass and add ice. Stir until proper dilution and strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with an orange peel.

name: Hophead Old Fashioned
instructions: Combine all ingredients into a mixing glass and stir until proper dilution. Strain into a chilled bucket glass over a large ice cube and garnish with a dukes lemon and dukes grapefruit zest.

name: Bacio Romana
instructions: Combine all ingredients into mixing glass. Add ice. Stir until very cold. Strain and fine strain to prevent ice chips in coupe glass. Garnish with orange twist entwined around a cinnamon stick.

name: 3 Degrees
instructions: Combine all ingredients with ice, shake, strain into martini glass, top with prosecco, garnish with sage.

name: Jack Cherry Sour (2001 Remix)
instructions:
Combine all ingredients, add ice, and shake. Double strain into an old fashioned glass and add fresh ice. Garnish with an orange slice (1/4 wheel) and Luxardo Cherry (both speared).

*0.25 - 0.50 oz Cane Sugar Syrup (to taste to allow variations in citrus flavor when batching)

name: Spring Forward
instructions: Combine all ingredients, minus the sparkling wine, into a tin. Shake and double strain into a flute glass. Top with a float of sparkling wine. Garnish with an edible flower petal.

name: Three Flips
instructions: Combine all ingredients, shake for 20 seconds. Add ice and shake again. Strain into a snifter. Garnish with shaved nutmeg.

name: Buenaventura
instructions: Combine all ingredients. Shake and strain into cocktail glass. Garnish with an orange peel.

name: Nieux Carré
instructions:
Combine all liquid ingredients in a chilled mixing glass with cracked ice and stir for 20 seconds.
Double strain the cocktail over a large cube placed in an old-fashioned glass.
Garnish with a lemon twist making sure to release all the essential oils out of the twist. Also make sure to rub the entire rim of the glass with the twist before dropping it into the cocktail.

name: L.E.S. Is More
instructions:
Combine all liquid ingredients in a highball or Collins glass with ice.
Stir lightly and garnish with a lemon twist making sure to release all the essential oils out of the twist. Also make sure to rub the entire rim of the glass with the twist before dropping it into the cocktail.

name: La Perle de Verre
instructions:
Combine all liquids with exception of sodas, sir together. Strain into collins glass with singular large cube. Top with sodas. Garnish with sliver of poached pear from syrup (kept chilled).

*Rose Poached Pear Syrup:
-- 1.5 C Rose (La Vielle Ferme or similar)
-- 1.5 C Water 
-- 2 C Sugar
-- 1 Large Lemon
-- 1 Half Orange
-- 1 tsp Ground Ginger (not fresh)
-- 1 tsp Vanilla
-- 3-5 Whole Cloves

Combine Rose (La Vielle Ferme or similar), water, and sugar. Bring to a simmer. Add zest of lemon and an orange, ground ginger, vanilla, and cloves. Peel and halve pears, set gently into liquid. Keep just under a simmer for 15 mins. Remove from heat, let cool at least 20 mins. Pears will keep for 3 days refrigerated in syrup. The syrup itself will last longer.

name: A Tall Tell
instructions: Combine all the ingredients except Prosecco. Add a large block of ice or large ice cubes and stir. Top with Prosecco. Garnish with orange wheels studded with cloves and float some mint sprigs in the bowl.

name: Cut and Run
instructions:
Combine all the ingredients in a mixing glass and stir for about 10 seconds. Strain in to an Old Fashioned glass or glass of your choosing. Add large ice cubes. Garnish with a lemon wheel, mint sprigs and a blackberry.

*Moroccan Mint Tea (Numi Tea Brand):
-- 1 Moroccan Mint Tea Bag
-- 8 oz. Boiling Water
 
Combine the tea bag and the boiling water in a cup or a pot. Let steep for 5 minutes. Remove the tea bag and place the tea in the fridge to cool. Label and date the container. Will keep for 1 week.

**Rich Honey Syrup:
-- 10 oz. Honey
-- 5 oz. Hot Water

Combine both ingredients together in a container and stir until fully incorporated. Label and date the container. Will keep in the fridge for 2 weeks.

name: The Stand By
instructions: Combine all the ingredients in an old-fashioned glass. Stir on the rocks. Garnish with lemon peel.

name: Second City Sour
instructions:
Combine bourbon, lemon juice, and syrup in a shaker and add cold ice. Shake until chilled well (10-30 sec. depending on the quality of the ice). Strain into a collins glass and slowly pour the vermouth into the glass. Garnish with a lemon twist. 

*Orange-scented Maple Syrup:
combine 6 oz. of grade B maple syrup, 6 oz. filtered water and the peel of 1 orange, with a minimum amount of pith in a non-reactive pan. 
Simmer for 10 mins. Remove from heat and allow it to cool to room temp. before using. 
Syrup will keep for 1 month if refrigerated.

name: California's Callin'
instructions: Combine Gin, Maraschino, juices, and bitters in an ice-filled mixing tin and shake for 20-30 seconds, or until well chilled. Strain over fresh ice into a Collins glass, top with the California Lager, and garnish with the grapefruit peel.

name: End of Winter
instructions: Combine H by HINE, King's Ginger Liqueur, and bitters over ice in a mixing glass. Stir until you reach proper dilution(15-20 seconds). Strain over a large ice rock in a bucket glass and garnish with Luxardo Cherries.

name: Hopped-Up Mule
instructions: Combine Hophead Vodka, Kings Ginger, and lime juice in a Collins glass and fill with ice. Top with ginger beer. Stir. Garnish with a lime wheel.

name: Bay City Shandy
instructions: Combine Hophead, lemon juice, Giffard Pamplemousse, honey syrup, and bitters in a shaker. Add ice and shake vigorously. Strain into a chilled glass. Fill the glass with beer.

name: San Fran Shandy
instructions: Combine Hophead, lemon juice, King's Ginger, honey syrup, egg white, and bitters in a shaker and dry shake to emulsify egg whites. Add ice and shake vigorously until proper dilution. Strain into a chilled fizz glass. Empty ice out of shakers and fill shaker with ale to create a froth. Pour this froth over the drink and garnish with half an orange wheel.

name: West Bank
instructions:
Combine ingredients and shake with ice. Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice. Grate nutmeg on top.

*Bywater Spice Mix: Simple syrup infused with Vietnamese cinnamon, licorice root, and clove

name: Hotaling White Manhattan
instructions: Combine ingredients in a mixing glass and stir with ice. Strain and serve up with an orange twist.

name: Coffey Grain Manhattan
instructions: Combine ingredients in a mixing glass with ice and stir. Strain into a coupe glass and garnish with a flamed orange peel.

name: Shadowcat
instructions: Combine ingredients in a pint glass, stir with ice to chill. Double strain into a coupe glass. Garnish with mint and lemon wedge flag.

name: San Francisco Harvest
instructions:
Combine ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake and double strain into a coupe glass. Garnish with nutmeg.

*Winter Spice Brown Sugar Syrup: 
-- 2 qt Brown Sugar
-- 2 qt Water
-- 3 Tbsp Ground Ginger
-- 3 Tbsp Ground Nutmeg
-- 1 Tbsp Clove

Heat and simmer the below ingredients in a medium pot for 15 minutes. Strain with cheesecloth.

name: Martinez
instructions: Combine ingredients in an ice-filled mixing glass and stir for 20 to 30 seconds, or until well chilled. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass and garnish with the lemon peel.

name: Negroni D'Amore
instructions: Combine ingredients in an old fashioned glass filled with ice, stir to combine, and garnish with orange peel.

name: Radice Amara
instructions: Combine ingredients in mixing glass, and stir with ice to dilution. Strain into a coupe cocktail glass. Garnish with a trimmed carrot ribbon.

name: Don't Rock the Boat Punch
instructions: Combine ingredients into a chilled punch bowl, ice and dilute appropriately, garnish seasonally.

name: Miyagikyo Kick
instructions:
Combine ingredients into a cocktail shaker. 
Shake with ice vigorously for 10-15 seconds. 
Strain into a chilled coupe glass. No garnish.

name: King's Toddy
instructions: Combine ingredients into a hot mug, top with hot water and garnish with a rough orange twist.

name: Picadilly
instructions: Combine ingredients into a mixing glass, add ice and shake until properly diluted. Double strain into a glass over fresh ice, garnish with a cucumber wheel.

name: Elgin Elixir
instructions: Combine ingredients with ice in a mixing glass and stir. Strain over a large cube in a rocks glass and garnish with an orange peel.

name: The Canevari Cocktail
instructions: Combine ingredients, stir until well chilled, and strain in to a cocktail glass. Serve with an orange twist.

name: Erasmus' Revenge
instructions: Combine ingredients. Pour over big cube and stir. Add the grapefruit peel.

name: The Sparkler
instructions: Combine Luxardo Bitters and Dolin Blanc. Stir in mixing glass until chilled. Strain into flute. Top with prosecco. Zest with grapefruit. Garnish with long grapefruit zest.

name: Shroud of the Inca
instructions:
Combine Luxardo, BarSol, and tincture. Ice, stir, and strain into chilled coupe. Top with Prosecco. Gently set candied blood orange wheel (thinly sliced) garnish into drink and serve.

*Castillian Tincture:
-- 750 ml Luxardo Grappa Euganea
-- 20 g Sliced Blanched Almond
-- 2 g Spanish Saffron
-- 3 Sliced Madagascan Vanilla Beans
-- 3 g Toasted Green Cardamom
-- 4 Lemon Peels (pith removed)
-- 2 Quartered Dried Pajarero Figs
-- 2 Quartered Quartered Medjool Dates

Combine all ingredients, gently agitate twice daily for 14 days. Fine filter and store.

**Candied Blood Orange Wheels:
-- 1 Blood Orange (sliced thin - approx. 10 slices/orange)
-- .5 c Natural Sugar
-- .25 c Water
-- 2 Tbsp Corn Syrup

Combine sugar, water, and corn syrup in shallow saucepan. Slow simmer on low heat approx. 10 min. When thickened mixture begins to cling to sides of pan, add orange slices. Allow to simmer for 5 more min. and remove from hardening syrup. Place slices on parchment-lined oven rack. Air dry overnight, then bake 10 min. at 400°F (or dehydrate them).

name: New Beginnings
instructions:
Combine Luxardo, cachaca, gin, coconut water, tincture and almond extract in a yari (mixing glass). Add ice. Stir for proper dilution (30-40 revolutions). Pour in collins glass over a clarified ice sphere. Top cocktail with Fever-Tree. Add 3 almond slices for garnish.

*Toasted Coconut Tincture:
-- 4 oz (by weight) Semisweet Coconut Flakes
-- High-Proof Neutral Grain Spirit (preferably Everclear)

Lightly toast semisweet coconut flakes. Put in a small mason jar. Fill jar with high-proof neutral grain spirit. Let sit for 3 days then strain off.

name: Fuji-La
instructions:
Combine Luxardo, gin, apple water and citric acid solution into a shaker tin and shake to chill. Double strain into a coupe glass and garnish with a slice of Fuji apple lightly dusted with cinnamon.

*Apple Water:
-- 1 Fuji Apple
-- 2 c Water

Slice Fuji apple and combine with water. Vacuum seal and place in a sous-vide for 3-4 hrs at 130°F. Strain apples; water is ready for use.

**Citric Acid Solution:
-- 1 ml gram Citric Acid
-- 3 ml Water

Combine citric acid and water. Stir until dissolved.

name: The Cliffdiver
instructions: Combine rum, curacao, violettes, and lime juice in a shaker, add ice and shake vigorously. Double strain into a chilled collins glass. Add fresh ice in the collins glass and top with chilled ginger beer. Float 2-3 dashes of Angostura Bitters on top and garnish with a grilled pineapple wedge on rim of glass, and two Luxardo Cherries speared through a toothpick, and edible orchid placed on top hanging from the rim.

name: Irish Goodbye
instructions: Combine the coffee and whiskey in a preheated cup. Top with Irish cream and pour the absinthe over the back of a spoon to float. Garnish.

name: Beachcomber
instructions: Combine the ingredients in a Boston shaker or cocktail shaker. Add ice and shake vigorously for 10 seconds. Strain the contents into a chilled cocktail glass.

name: Old Tom and The Sea
instructions:
Combine the ingredients. Shake and strain over ice. Lemon and/or cherry flag garnish.

Add soda water if you'd like to water it down.

name: Salty Swede
instructions: Combine the Karlsson's Gold Vodka and grapefruit in a shaker with ice. Shake for 15 seconds and strain over fresh ice in an old-fashioned glass that has a salted rim. Top the cocktail with the club soda.

name: The Last Tango
instructions:
Combine the lime, elderflower, tincture and cherries into a shaker tin. Muddle the cherries and then add the Junipero Gin. Shake with ice. Double strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Garnish with a lime wheel.

*Chili Tincture:
-- Chiles (Habaneros or Bird’s eye Chiles recommended)
-- Grain Alcohol or Vodka

Fill a small jar with stemmed hot chili peppers and cover with grain alcohol or high-proof vodka. Let soak for 3 days; strain.

name: Pink & Black
instructions: Combine the Pink Pigeon, the cola, and the lime juice, in a tall Collins glass full of ice cubes.

name: Pink & Ginger
instructions: Combine the Pink Pigeon, the ginger beer, and the lime juice, in a tall Collins glass full of ice cubes.

name: A Regal Air
instructions: Combine these ingredients and roll gently over ice and strain into a martini glass. Top with pumpkin foam.

name: King's Mule
instructions:
Combine vodka, ginger liqueur, lemon juice, ginger-cinnamon syrup and bitters in tins with ice and shake. Double strain into collins glass over fresh ice and top with club soda.

*Ginger-Cinnamon Syrup: 
Equal parts demerara sugar, clover honey, fresh ginger juice, water. Combine demerara sugar and clover honey and top with boiling water. Stir to dissolve. Once cooled add fresh ginger juice and stir to mix.

name: Caipirinha
instructions: Cut a lime in 4 pieces and put them in a glass. Add 1-2 tablespoons of sugar and crush with a pestle. Add Ypiòca. Fill with crushed ice. Stir well and enjoy!

name: Bubbly Bird
instructions: Dash each bitter and the Fernet over sugar cube in base of Champagne glass so that the sugar is fully saturated with mixture. Add Pink Pigeon Rum and top with chilled Champagne. Garnish with a twist of lemon.

name: Framboise de Pigeon
instructions: Double strain into a martini glass.

name: Far From the Tree
instructions: Dry Shake (no ice), Wet Shake (with ice) & Double Strain.

name: Luxardo Sour
instructions: Dry shake all ingredients for 20-30 seconds. Then shake with ice. Double strain over fresh ice in an old fashioned glass. Garnish with wide lemon swath.

name: The Molino
instructions: Dry shake all ingredients. Hard shake with ice. Double strain into small pilsner glass. Garnish with Luxardo Cherry.

name: The King's Egg Nog
instructions: Dry shake and then shake with hand cracked ice. Serve in a goblet with grated nutmeg.

name: White Walker Flip
instructions: Dry shake whole egg until emulsified. Then add Kold Draft ice and all ingredients except bitters and shake vigorously until cold. Strain into chilled royal coupette and garnish with apple wheels.

name: Hibiscus Pisco Sour
instructions: Dry shake, add ice and shake again. Strain into a coupe glass.

name: Lux Japanese Lemonade
instructions: Dry Shake, add ice and shake for 10 seconds. Double strain into a Copita Glass. Garnish with Dill.

name: Flip and Flight
instructions: Dry shake, shake with cubed ice, double strain into chilled glass coated in vanilla-salt solution.

name: Dry Mauritian Sour
instructions: Dry shake, shake with cubed ice, double strain into chilled glass.

name: Platonic Snuggle
instructions: Dry shake, strain, top with porter.

name: The Brightside
instructions: Dry shake, then serve old-fashioned glass with crushed ice. Drizzle Bitter on top to finish.

name: The King's Froth & Frizz
instructions:
Dry shake, then shake with ice. Double strain into flute or sour glass. Top with a Saison.

Zest orange over top and garnish with a star anise.

name: The Queen's A Redhead
instructions: Dry shake, then shake with ice. Double strain over cubed ice.

name: Delicious Sour
instructions: Dry shake. Add ice, shake. Double strain into a frozen coupe glass. Garnish with lemon twist or dehydrated apple slice.

name: Jalapeño Sour
instructions:
dryshake/shake/dblrocks

garnish jalapeño slice

name: Hanny's Hot Apple Toddy
instructions: Fill a 10-oz mug with boiling water. When the mug is hot, pour out the water and add the amaretto and cider. Top with a layer of cream. Grate fresh cinnamon on top as a garnish.

name: The Alpine Mist
instructions: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add all ingredients except the garnish and shake well. Stain into a very chilled martini glass (martini glass should have a sugar rim on half of the glass) and top with a splash of ginger beer. Garnish with a mint leaf and orange peel. Right before serving drop in a chip of dry ice for the mist.

name: Division Bell
instructions: Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add ingredients and shake well. Strain into a chilled coupe or martini glass and garnish with the grapefruit twist.

name: Golden Gun
instructions:
Fill a collins or highball glass with cracked or cubed ice. Add all the ingredients to a cocktail shaker filled with cracked or cubed ice. Shake and strain into the glass.

*SC Demerara Syrup:
-- 2 C Water
-- 1 C Demerara Sugar
-- 3 C Granulated Sugar

Bring water to boil in a saucepan over high heat. Add the demerara sugar and stir vigorously with a whisk (or use an immersion blender) until sugar is dissolved, about 1 minute (the water should become clear). Add granulated sugar and stir vigorously until dissolved, about 1 minute. Remove from heat and let cool.

name: Grown-Up Shirley Temple
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice, and build the syrup, lemon juice, and soda water. Stir, and garnish with a Luxardo Maraschino Cherry.

name: Japanese Cocktail
instructions: Fill a mixing glass half full with ice. Add cognac, orgeat, and bitters. Stir well. Strain into chilled cocktail glass. Twist lemon peel over the drink and drop in as a garnish.

name: Vinterpäls
instructions: Fill a mug with very hot water to warm the glass up. Once the glass is hot, empty it and add all the liquid ingredients. Stir well until the honey is incorporated. Garnish with the clove studded orange peel and enjoy.

name: A Southern Jackass
instructions:
Fill a shaker with an old fashioned glass worth of ice. Add all ingredients except for the club soda. Shake vigorously for 20 seconds, then dump into old fashioned glass. Set lemon wheel and sliver of cinnamon-candied ginger on top then top with club soda.

*Cinnamon Candied Ginger Syrup:
Combine 1/4 lb. peeled ginger root (sliced 1/8" on a 45-degree bias using a mandolin), 2 cups of water, and 4 cinnamon sticks in a pot (or pan) and bring to cusp of a light simmer. Continue to barely simmer for 30 minutes. Add 1 cup of demerara sugar and stir until fully dissolved. Lower heat and keep just below simmering. Continue to heat until almost all liquid is reduced and remaining sugar is just beginning to caramelize (2-3 hours). Remove from heat and spoon slivers of ginger (along with a generous dollop of now thick syrup) onto a sheet of parchment or wax paper. Let cool for at least 2 hours then store layered on the paper sealed in a ziplock bag. Remove cinnamon sticks and reserve syrup.

name: Mayan Sacrifice Champagne Cocktail
instructions:
Fill Chinaco stemmed flute with prosecco, cava or other dry sparkling.
Garnish with Luxardo Cherry pinned inside of lemon peel and rested across rim.
The cherry garnish which will be EVER so delicately laid across the top of the glass, should flame in my opinion. You can dip the in high-proof spirits and light it up!

name: Pacific Kick
instructions: Fill shaker with ingredients and ice. Shake. Strain into old fashioned or rocks glass filled with ice.

name: Citrus Quo (Adapted)
instructions: Fill up a stem-less wine glass with ice and pour the ingredients over, stir and serve.

name: The Mayan Premonition
instructions: Fill up a stemmed wine glass with ice then pour the ingredients, stir and serve.

name: Club Med
instructions: Fill up a wine glass with ice then pour the ingredients, stir and serve.

name: Pisco Sour
instructions: First, dry shake all ingredients. Then, shake all ingredients vigorous with ice and strain into a cocktail glass. Garnish with 2-3 drops of Angostura bitters on top of the foam.

name: Jamaica Paloma
instructions: Flash shake, strain onto fresh ice, top with Mexican Squirt. Salted starfruit garnish.

name: Ypioca Chile Punch
instructions: For a punch, combine Ypioca, lime, agave and Ancho Reyes together. Add fresh pineapple chunks, 3 thin sliced chiles (seeds included) and 1 star anise to garnish.

name: Prata Punch
instructions:
For punch, combine Ypioca, Lillet Rose, guava nectar, lime and agave. Top with Rosa Regale sparkling and add sliced strawberries and thin-sliced lime wheels to float as garnish.

For individual cocktails, muddle 2-3 strawberry slices w/ agave and add Ypioca, guava and Lillet. Add ice and shake to chill/mix. Strain over ice and top with champagne. Garnish with thin-sliced strawberry. Remember, this is an aperitif style so it's meant to be refreshing but not necessarily sweet.

name: Engaged and Cant-Aloupe
instructions:
Garnish with 3 drops of Angostura Bitters over foam.

*Beet Sugar Water:
-- 50% Beet Sugar
-- 50% Water

name: Mauritian Made
instructions: Garnish: mint and lime wheel

name: Hotaling Old Tom Gin
instructions:
Garnished with orange peel and star anise. Served over the rocks in a stemless wine glass.

*7-Spice Syrup:
-- Pink Peppercorn
-- Star Anise
-- Sumac
-- Cinnamon
-- Nutmeg
-- Coriander
-- Cardamom

name: Hotaling Seaside Shandy
instructions: Genevieve is Hotaling's interpretation of genever, an ancient and mysterious gin style. The most common way to consume genever has always been straight. If you are planning to set sail for the day, picnic or do something else to enjoy the great outdoors, we recommend throwing a bottle of Genevieve in the freezer the evening before your outing and moving it to the cooler day of. We sip it straight while enjoying a lager. Glassware is dealer's choice, but cordial glasses are elegant and fun.

name: Blind Luck
instructions:
Hard vigorous shake with high density ice for 5-6 seconds. Hawthorne and fine strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with a mint sprig spanked and lightly rubbed around the edge of the glass. Raspberry garnish and mint sprig attached to the raspberry. Channel zest lemon bath over the glass.

*Strawberry-Raspberry Turbinado "Sugar in the Raw" Simple Syrup:
-- 2 C Water
-- 2 C Sugar in the Raw Turbinado Sugar
-- 15 Raspberries
-- 3 Strawberries, quartered
-- 3 Mint Leaves

As the water boils, slowly add sugar a few spoon fulls at a time and whisk vigorously into the boiling water until all 2 cups have been dissolved. Continue to keep on high heat for 5 mins.
Then take raspberries, strawberries, and mint leaves and macerate all components together and add into the boiling simple syrup.
Reduce heat to low-medium heat for the next 15 mins.
After that allow simple syrup to cool down and rest for additional 15 mins. 
Double strain the simple syrup through fine strainers until all fruit, seeds and mint leaves are strained out.  Allow to cool.

name: The Naughty & Nice
instructions:
Heat cider to 180°F. In a 5 oz cup pour the scotch and drambuie. Add the heated cider. Stir in the spiced butter. Top with 3 drops bitters.

For the garnish, add 2 small cocktail stirrers, hang cookie off of the stirrer so that it leans off of the side of the cup.

*Spiced Butter:
-- 1 half Stick Butter
-- 1 tbsp Pumpkin Pie Spice
-- .5 tbsp Brown Sugar
-- .5 tsp Cayenne Pepper

Soften butter, add spices and mix with a spoon until all ingredients are combined.

**Chrusciki Cookies:
-- 1 c All-purpose Flour
-- 3 Egg Yolks
-- 3 tbsp Sour Cream
-- 1 tsp Vanilla Extract
-- 1 tbsp Dark Rum
-- 1 pinch Salt

Using a mixer, incorporate egg yolks into sour cream, add vanilla and rum, kneed in all-purpose flower and salt to taste.
Divide dough in half on a floured board and let rest for 20 min. Working with half the dough at a time, roll out to 1/8 in. thickness, cut into 2 in. wide strips, cut these strips on the diagonal at 4 in. intervals.
Heat 2 in. of vegetable oil in a large deep skillet to 350°F. Make a slit in the center of each strip of dough. Pull one end through the slit to form a bow.
Fry 6 Chrusciki at a time for 1 min. or less per side till golden brown.
Drain on a paper towel and dust with confectioners sugar.

name: The King's Cranberry Sauce
instructions: Heat cranberries, sugar, OJ and King's Ginger over medium heat. Place the peppercorns, allspice, cloves and cinnamon in a piece of cheesecloth with the end tied. Add to pot. Cook stirring often until cranberries soften about 25 to 30 min. Refrigerate for 1 hour before serving.

name: Mexican Sazerac
instructions: Ice: Large block or spherical

name: Roasted Cherry Colada
instructions: In a blender, combine white rum, Luxardo, coconut milk, lime juice, sugar, bitters, 3 cherries, and 1 cup ice and purée until smooth; pour into a tiki glass and top with black rum. Place halved cherry, cut-side up, on top of cocktail and fill with Everclear before lighting cherry on fire.

name: Karlsson's Kold Brew
instructions: In a Boston shaker combine all liquid ingredients (not the foam) with cracked ice and shake vigorously for 20 seconds. Double strain the cocktail into an espresso cup. Using an aero-latte frothing whip froth the coffee infused soy milk and spoon the froth over the cocktail. Sprinkle with a little espresso powder.

name: Root Down
instructions: In a Boston shaker combine all liquid ingredients except the ginger beer with whole hard ice and shake vigorously for 30 seconds. Double strain the cocktail over whole hard ice cubes in a 13 oz. rocks glass and top with the ginger beer. Garnish with the licorice root.

name: Chilli Vanilli
instructions: In a Boston shaker combine all liquid ingredients in a shaker without ice and dry shake for 10-12 shakes to blend the egg whites. Once blended add cracked ice and shake vigorously for 30 seconds and strain into a frozen 4.5 oz. coupe glass. Let the cocktail settle and sprinkle with the vanilla powder.

name: Pear and Cranberry Fizz
instructions: In a Boston shaker combine and muddle the fresh lemon juice and cranberries. Add the remaining liquid ingredients except the club soda with hard ice and shake vigorously for 20 seconds pour directly into a highball add a little more ice to fill the glass and top with the club soda. Garnish with a few pear slices and fresh cranberries.

name: Asian Affair
instructions:
In a Boston shaker, combine all liquid ingredients except the ginger beer with cracked ice and shake vigorously for 30 seconds. Double strain over ice into a 16 oz. highball or Collins glass and top with the ginger beer. Garnish with the candied ginger using 2 toothpicks placed on the rim of the glass.

*Thai Basil Syrup:
-- 1 C Chopped Thai Basil
-- 2 C Granulated Sugar
-- 1 C Water

In a 1 qt. saucepot bring the water and sugar to boil and stir until sugar is completely dissolved. Add the chopped Thai basil to the pot and remove from heat. Let the basil steep for 1 hour or until syrup is room temperature. Strain the basil out of the syrup using a fine mesh sieve and store covered in fridge.

name: Pumpkin and Spice
instructions:
In a Boston shaker, combine all liquid ingredients except the ginger beer. Shake vigorously for 25 seconds and strain over fresh ice in a rocks glass. Top with the ginger beer and grate the fresh nutmeg over the top.

*Oatmeal-Infused Karlsson's Gold Vodka:
-- 750 ml Karlsson's Gold Vodka
-- 2 C Raw Oats

Using a large sauté pan toast the oats for about 10 minutes constantly watching them and making sure they don't burn. Once aromatic and have a touch of color add the oats to a mason jar with the vodka and let infuse for 12-24 hours. Strain and press out all the vodka from the oats. Return the liquid to the bottle and store in a cool dark place.

name: Tambourine
instructions: In a Boston shaker, combine all liquid ingredients with cracked ice and shake vigorously for 30 seconds. Double strain into a frozen 4.5 oz. coupe glass. Garnish with 1/2 piece kaffir lime leaf. (Clap the leaf several times before placing on top of the cocktail to release the aromatics.)

name: Eden's Apple
instructions: In a Boston shaker, combine all liquid ingredients with cracked ice and shake vigorously for 30 seconds. Double strain into a frozen 4.5 oz. coupe glass. Garnish with the apple slice. Can also be served warm.

name: Swedish 75
instructions: In a Boston shaker, combine the Karlsson's and the lemon curd with whole hard ice and shake vigorously for 30-40 seconds. Add the champagne to the shaker, stir until mixed well and double strain the cocktail into a 4.5 oz. coupe glass. Garnish the cocktail with a lemon twist making sure to release all the essential oils into the glass. Rub the entire rim with the twist before dropping the twist into the glass.

name: Barbary Coast Bulldog
instructions: In a bucket glass, combine Hophead, coffee liqueur, and cream. Add Ice and give a quick stir. Top with cold Mexican Coke and garnish with a Luxardo Cherry.

name: Apple Shrub Spritz
instructions: In a chilled mixing glass combine the Karlsson's gold vodka and the spiced apple shrub with cracked ice and stir for 20 seconds. Add the chilled club soda to the mixing glass, stir just until mixed and strain into a frozen champagne flute. Garnish with two apple slices placed on the rim.

name: Goat Town Swizzle
instructions: In a collins glass, add ingredients beginning with orgeat and ending with rye. Fill glass half full with minted crushed ice. Begin "swizzling" cocktail with stick until cocktail is blended and ice forms on outside of glass. Top with more minted crushed ice and garnish with lemon and mint.

name: The No.3 Winter Cup
instructions: In a highball glass full of ice add No.3, King's Ginger and red vermouth. Top up with the lemonade and serve with a slice of ginger and a wedge of pink grapefruit.

name: La La Land
instructions: In a mixing glass muddle mint, add all other ingredients and ice, shake, fine strain into a double old fashioned glass over fresh cracked ice. Garnish with cucumber slices and raspberries.

name: Vieux Carré
instructions: In a mixing glass, add all ingredients, then fill halfway with ice. Stir with a barspoon until chilled, about 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Rub the lemon twist around the rim, drop it in the cocktail, and serve.

name: Mack's Apple
instructions:
In a mixing glass, add ice and shake with all of the ingredients. Strain over new ice (large sphere ice cube) and serve in old fashioned glass and grate fresh cinnamon over the ice.

*Apple Cider Reduction: Boil apple cider until it reduces in volume by half.

**Cinnamon-Chipotle Syrup: Bring 1 cup sugar, 1 cup water and 3 cinnamon sticks to boil, take off heat and let steep for 20mins, add chipotle powder and mix well.

name: The Ladybird
instructions: In a mixing glass, add the first three ingredients over ice. Stir to chill. In a separate coupe glass, add .5 oz of Luxardo Fernet and swirl glass until it is fully coated with the Fernet. Strain chilled cocktail from mixing glass into the Fernet-rinsed coupe (discard excess fernet). Garnish by placing a Luxardo Cherry into the bottom of the glass.

name: Passionate Pigeon
instructions: In a mixing glass, combine Pink Pigeon Rum, Lychee Juice and the juice from 1/2 a lime. Add the pulp of one passion fruit (to prep passion fruit: cut in half, setting aside a small slice for garnish and scoop out the pulp and drop both the pulp and peel into the glass). Add ice and shake vigorously. Pour in a tall glass over ice, top with ginger beer.  Garnish with the passion fruit wedge to serve.

name: Mayan Decadence
instructions: In a pint glass pour all the ingredients, stir and strain into a bucket glass 3/4 full of ice and garnish with an orange peel.

name: The No.3 Winter Punch
instructions: In a punch bowl add ingredients per person.

name: Xmas in Juniper
instructions:
In a shaker mix all ingredients discarding the beer and dry shake vigorously to activate the egg whites. Add ice and shake for about 10 sec and double strain into 8 oz glass and top with beer. Garnish with a marigold flower.

*Spiced Apricot Syrup:
-- Organic Earl Grey Tea
-- Green Cardamom
-- Juniper Berries

name: Muddled Mission
instructions: In a shaker, gently muddle the strawberry. Add the remaining ingredients and shake with ice. Strain into a coupe. Garnish with strawberry.

name: The Southside
instructions: In a shaker, muddle the mint and simple syrup. Add the lemon juice and gin, and fill with ice. Shake, and double-strain into a cocktail glass. Garnish with a mint sprig.

name: Apricot Blush
instructions: In a tall glass muddle the mint and plum with fresh sweet n' sour, pour the liquors then fill up with ice and top with Prosecco.

name: Nikka Coffey Malt Old Fashioned
instructions: In an old fashioned glass, muddle the lemon peel with the bitters and demerara syrup, just enough to bruise the peel and release its oils. Add Coffey Malt and ice, and stir for 20 to 30 seconds, or until well chilled.

name: Honey Basil Smash
instructions: In collins glass, gently muddle honey, lime and basil leaves. Add English Harbour rum and ice and stir. Top with ginger ale.

name: Nikka Coffey Grain Perfect Manhattan
instructions: In mixing glass or cocktail shaker filled with ice, combine whiskey, vermouth, and bitters. Stir well, about 20 seconds, then strain into cocktail glass. Add cherry and serve.

name: Juniperotivo
instructions: In mixing glass, using a spoon, crush 2 mint leaves in the simple syrup, and add gin, lime juice and pomegranate syrup. Pour into ice-filled shaker. Shake well and strain into chilled cocktail glass. Float remaining 2 mint leaves for garnish.

name: Desmond's Choice
instructions: In old fashioned glass, lightly muddle orange zest with bitters and orange curacao.  Add bourbon and creme de cacao then stir with ice for proper dilution (depending on ice) and mixing of ingredients.

name: Cafe Creole
instructions:
In one shaker, mix heavy cream and simple syrup and dry shake vigorously until it's whipped.
In another, shake Pink Pigeon Rum and Luxardo Espresso with ice.  Pour into a coupe, and then carefully top with the cream.  It should layer perfectly.  The vanilla complexity of the rum pairs awesomely with coffee and cream.

name: La Nueva Moda (The New Fashion)
instructions:
In rocks glass, make an emulsion by using a glass muddler (wood muddlers absorb the ingredients too much). Place sugar cubes in glass. Add mezcal onto the cubes. Add bitters, onto cubes. Add drops of agave nectar, onto cubes. Add Luxardo, onto cubes. Muddle well to make a "syrup" paying attention the blending of all ingredients. 
Take the long orange peel and twist all the essential oils into glass. Leave peel in glass. 
Place Gläce Luxury Ice G-Cubed in glass. Rotate Gläce G-Cubed so that it becomes coated and chills the "syrup" swirl around the inside of glass. Pour the Anejo Tequila over Gläce G-Cubed. Stir with glass swizzle. Garnish with 3 Luxardo Cherries, speared.

name: And The Cherry On Top Is...
instructions: In the bottom of a cocktail shaker, muddle pitted cherries if using. Add Luxardo Maraschino, vodka, fresh lemon juice and simple syrup. Shake hard, then double strain (though a fine mesh strainer) into a tall glass with fresh ice. Add a splash of soda and stick a cherry on top.

name: Phad Thai
instructions:
In the small tin of a Boston shaker set, combine the cilantro sprigs, thai chili, yuzu juice, simple syrup and a small piece of ice. Muddle these ingredients well. Add the remaining liquid ingredients to the shaker with cracked ice and shake vigorously for 20 seconds. 
Double strain the cocktail over ice into a 13 oz. rocks glass. 
Garnish with the Thai chili piece and the cilantro sprig.

name: The Swedish
instructions: Juice potato and strain into a nearby usable container (must be done as close to drink making process as possible since oxidization will occur quickly). Add vodka, potato juice, lemon, Benedictine, cinnamon-honey syrup, and salt and pepper to mixing glass. Add ice, and shake and fine strain over fresh ice.

name: Spring Cleanse
instructions:
Juice the ingredients and pour into a chilled glass (do not use ice or the drink will lose body).

Top with Pastis Prado or absinthe and garnish with a sprig of mint.

name: The King's Winter Cup
instructions: Method:  Simmer on a gentle heat for 30 minutes and garnish with a cinnamon stick

name: Prickly Pear Margarita
instructions: Mint salt on the rim. Garnish with mint sprig.

name: The Sixth Sense
instructions: Mix all ingredients in a blender until smooth and icy. Pout into a glass and top with 3 dashes of angostura bitters.

name: Pink Punch
instructions: Mix all ingredients in a large pitcher, add ice, and enjoy!

name: The 42nd Round
instructions:
Mix all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir for 15-20 secs depending on the type of ice. Strain over a large rock and garnish with an orange peel expressing the oils over the cocktail.

*Anchor Christmas Ale 2016 w/ Honey (reduction) 2:1 (2-parts Beer to 1-part Honey)

name: The Nutty Sensation
instructions:
Mix all ingredients in a tin except for the walnut oil. Utilizing the throwing technique, throw the mixture from one vessel to another vessel until chilled to air out the ingredients. Coat coupe glass with walnut oil and pour the mixture. Garnish with a dehydrated orange.

*Oloroso & Moscatel Blend:
-- 2 parts César Florido Moscatel Special
-- 1 part Lustau Oloroso Sherry

Blend ingredients.

name: Maria Canevari Punch
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.

name: Trip to Jarnac
instructions: Mix all ingredients together.

name: No.3 Negroni
instructions: Mix all the liquid contents together in a heavy-bottomed tumbler with ice. Stir. Add a slice of orange and lemon zest to decorate.

name: Clover Holiday Cocktail
instructions: Mix all together and shake and double strain into a coupe without ice. *Bay Leaf-Infused Honey Syrup: • 3 cups honey to 1 cup water • 4 bay leaves, crumbled Bring to a boil. Stir until honey completely dissolves in water. Turn off heat and allow 2 hours to cool.

name: Barrel Aged Ginger Negroni
instructions: Mix equal parts of the 4 spirits and age in a 5 liter oak barrel for 30 days. Transfer into glass bottles and rest for a minimum of 3 days. Make giant (2" x 2") ice cubes with an orange peel frozen inside. Pour negroni on ice cube and serve.

name: Amaretto Bourbon Punch
instructions: Mix ingredients in the punch bowl.

name: Merry Sherry Cobbler
instructions: Mix ingredients together then pour over crushed ice into a Julep tin. Dust with salt, sugar, and cinnamon.

name: French 75
instructions: Mix the gin or cognac, lemon juice and syrup in a cocktail shaker. Pour the contents into a Champagne flute. Top up with Champagne. Add a Luxardo Cherry or a peel of lemon.

name: A Mi Manera (My Way) Pisco Punch
instructions: Mix the Pisco, apricot liqueur and citric acid ingredients together in a punch bowl and allow to rest for 45 minutes. Place a large 2.2 lb. ice block 10 minutes before serving. Scoop the pineapple sorbet in a punch glass and fill up with the mix, top up with mint-flavored soda. Garnish with candied pineapple wheel from Dress the Drink.

name: You're Bitter Than That
instructions: Mount all ingredients in a shaker tin. Add ice. Hard shake. Double strain into a chilled coupe glass. Garnish with orange twist.

name: Cocao Martini
instructions: Muddle 3 cacao beans in a mixing glass, add vodka, ginger-brown sugar, and Bitter Beast Hella Hula bitters. Add ice and vigorously shake to incorporate cacao flavor. Fine strain into a martini glass. Garnish with a cocao bean.

name: On the Flipside
instructions: Muddle 3-4 cucumber slices and a couple mint leaves. Shake and strain into martini or coupe glass. Garnish with lime or mint or cucumber.

name: Farmer's Gold
instructions: Muddle 4 basil leaves, 16 peppercorns and simple syrup in shaker tin. Add all other ingredients except final basil leaf. Add ice. Shake vigorously. Fine strain into chilled cocktail glass. Express lemon oil from lemon swath over drink. Garnish with lemon swath and final basil leaf.

name: Winter Swizzle
instructions:
Muddle a small handful of fresh mint leaves, ¾ oz simple syrup (equal parts sugar and water) 
1 oz fresh lime juice.
Add crushed ice to the glass to fill half way.
pour in 2 oz of Pink Pigeon.
Swizzle to incorporate ingredients and slowly add in the rest of the crushed ice.

name: Lost at Sea
instructions: Muddle basil in mixing glass, add all ingredients except club soda. Shake and strain into a collins glass, top with club soda.  Top with crushed ice and garnish with a basil sprig.

name: Feingold
instructions: Muddle cherry in liquid ingredients. Shake, double strain over cracked ice. Garnish with two more cherries on a pick with an orange flag.

name: Lovage In The Garden
instructions: Muddle everything in a tin except Hophead. Then add Hophead and ice and shake and strain into a rocks glass with ice and garnish with a lovage sprig.

name: Georgia Old Fashioned
instructions: Muddle fruit, bitters and syrup. Add ice (1 large cube if available). Top with Whisky. Long stir.

name: Stroll Through the Park
instructions: Muddle mint and cucumber with the simple syrup in a shaker tin. Combine the remainder of the ingredients in the tin. Shake vigorously. Double strain with a fine mesh strainer into a collins glass. Fill with ice and garnish with a cucumber slice.

name: Naughty Nikka
instructions: Muddle Orange Slice. Add Nikka. Add Ginger Syrup. Stir over Ice. Add bitters.

name: Masataka's Vision
instructions: Muddle orange twist with sugar. Add ice. Pour ingredients over ice. Add bitters

name: Ginger Pigeon
instructions: Muddle pineapple and ginger root, lemon juice and simple syrup, then add Aperitivo and rum, shake well, over ice and top up with Ginger beer.

name: Spice and Stormy
instructions: Muddle pineapple and ginger root, lemon juice and simple syrup. Add rum. Shake well, over ice and top with ginger beer and Luxardo Bitter

name: Stone Fruit Patch V2
instructions: Muddle raspberries in mixing glass. Add remainder of ingredients in a mixing glass with fresh ice. Shake vigorously and strain into a well chilled coupe. Top off with sparkling wine. Garnish with raspberry and mint.

name: Stone Fruit Patch V1
instructions: Muddle raspberries in mixing glass. Add remainder of ingredients in a mixing glass with fresh ice. Shake vigorously strain over fresh ice. Garnish with raspberry and mint.

name: Mint Garden
instructions:
Muddle red pepper and mint. Add all other ingredients. Shake well over ice and strain into a chilled martini glass.
Garnish with red pepper slice and mint sprig.

name: Sabotage
instructions: Muddle sage and Thyme. Stirred, over ice in single rocks glass. Burnt orange, and thyme sprig to garnish.

name: Rhapsody in Red
instructions: Muddle strawberries in a shaker with agave nectar, add lime juice, hibiscus tea, and Chinaco Blanco. Top with sparkling water.

name: Cherry Old Fashioned
instructions:
Muddle sugar and 1 roasted cherry in a rocks glass until the sugar has dissolved; add vodka, Amaro, Maraschino, and bitters and stir. Add 1 large ice cube and garnish with half a roasted cherry and the tarragon.

Cherry-Infused Vodka:
- 1 cup pitted cherries, quartered
- 1 cup vodka
Combine cherries and vodka in a bottle; cover, and let sit to meld the flavors for 3-5 days.

name: Basil Gin and Tonic
instructions: Muddle the basil with the lime juice, then add tonic water. Fill glass with ice and stir in the gin.

name: Kumquat Smash
instructions: Muddle the fruit with syrup in a mixing glass. Add ice and shake vigorously. Pour all ingredients(do not strain) in a tumbler glass. Add a float of Mango flavored Rum.

name: Mystique Green (Adapted)
instructions: Muddle the kiwi pulp with lime juice in a shaker tin. Add ice and liquors and shake vigorously. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass.

name: Nara Mule
instructions: Muddle the kumquats and lychee in a cocktail shaker. Add the whiskey, King's Ginger, lemon juice, syrup and white egg, and shake very hard. Double strain into a highball glass filled with ice spheres.

name: The Reserved King
instructions: Muddle the lemon peel and bitters with the King’s Ginger to release the oils.  Add scotch, stir, and enjoy.

name: Buccaneer
instructions: Muddle the pineapple adding more or less simple syrup as needed and depending on sweetness of the pineapple. Add all other ingredients. Give the mixture a hard shake. Double strain into chilled coupe.

name: Sage & Sound
instructions:
Muddle the sage in a mixing tin. Add the remaining ingredients and dry shake (no ice) for 15 seconds. Add ice and shake another 15 seconds to chill down the drink. Double strain into a large coupe glass. Garnish with a whole star anise floated in the middle of the egg white.

*Honey Cinnamon Syrup:
-- 16 oz Honey
-- 2 Cinnamon Sticks
-- 8 oz Water
Combine all the ingredients together in a pot. Place on the stove and bring to a simmer, stirring the honey so it doesn’t burn and let the pot simmer for 20 minutes. Remove from heat and place on an ice bath. Place in a container and label and date it. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

name: Japonais No.3
instructions: Muddle two cucumber slices, two raspberries and simple syrup in a highball, add ice. Shake gin, ginger liqueur and orange juice with ice. Strain into highball glass.  Garnish with a candied ginger stick.

name: PDA
instructions: Muddled ginger root. Shaken, served up, cinnamon stick and apple to garnish.

name: Apples & Grapes
instructions: On the rocks with twist.

name: The Proverb
instructions: Place all ingredients in a mixing glass. Add ice and stir until proper dilution. Strain over a large hand cut ice cube placed in a double rocks glass. Garnish with a Luxardo Maraschino Cherry on a pick.

name: Raspberry Batida
instructions: Place all ingredients in a mixing tin, muddle fresh raspberries, add ice and shake until tin is frosty. Strain into a chilled highball glass. Garnish with orange wedge.

name: Ode to Kinshi
instructions: Place all ingredients into a Yarai mixing glass with ice and stir. Strain into a coupe. Garnish with an apple slice.

name: Junipero Gin and Tonic
instructions:
Place ice cubes in a rocks glass. Add ingredients. Garnish with your choice of: rosemary, basil, lavender, edible flowers, or a lemon, lime or orange twist.

*Tonic Water Ice Cubes:
Fill an ice cube tray with tonic water and freeze.

name: L'Aviation
instructions: Place ingredients in a shaker with ice and shake hard for seven seconds. Double strain into a chilled martini glass and garnish with lemon peel.

name: The Kings Mule
instructions: Place King's Ginger and orange bitters in a Champagne flute, and top with sparkling wine. No garnish.

name: The Bacchus Cocktail
instructions: Place the Concord grapes and blackberries in a shaker and muddle. Add the rest of the ingredients and ice. Shake, and strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with Concord grapes and lemon peel twisted to release oils.

name: Garrison's New Fashioned
instructions: Place the lemon peel in an Old Fashioned glass and muddle with bitters. In a mixing glass filled with ice, add the bourbon and apricot liqueur, then strain into the glass. Garnish with cherry and orange slice, and serve over an ice sphere.

name: Pina Del Este
instructions: Place the lime wedges and pinapple in a tumbler glass with gomme syrup and muddle briskly, then add ice and Amaretto. Stir well and serve with a float of Pisco.

name: Vida Verde (Adapted)
instructions: Place the mint and cucumber in a mixing glass and muddle briskly, then add sour mix and liquor. Shake with ice and double strain over ice into a stemmed glass.

name: Culebra
instructions: Place the mint in a Collins glass and muddle for a few seconds to release the oils, then add ice and ingredients, stir well and serve.

name: Morlacco Julep
