instructions: Place the mint in a tumbler glass and muddle briskly to release the essential oils, then add the cherry liqueur and the brandy. Fill up the glass with crushed ice, stir well and serve with a float of Luxardo Amaretto. Garnish with 4 sprigs of mint and 3 Luxardo Cherries.

name: Sorrentino
instructions: Place the thyme in a tumbler glass and muddle to release the oils, then add ice and ingredients, stir well and serve.

name: SOMA Fizz
instructions: Pour 2 oz. of Club Soda into a Collin’s Glass. Hard shake first fIve ingredients with ice for at 20 seconds. Slowly strain into the Collins glass. Garnish with an orange wedge.

name: The Ginger Royale
instructions: Pour a generous measure of The King's Ginger into a flute. Add chilled Champagne to taste.

name: The Royal King
instructions: Pour a generous measure of The King's Ginger into a flute. Add chilled Champagne to taste.

name: Brazilian Mai Tai
instructions: Pour all ingredients and shake in tin. Pour into bucket. Top with rum float, then garnish.

name: Sorriso (Adapted)
instructions: Pour all ingredients in a mixing glass filled with ice. Stir well and strain into a chilled cocktail glass.

name: Pacific Breeze
instructions: Pour all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake and strain over ice into a Collins or Pilsner glass.

name: Heart Breaker
instructions: Pour all ingredients into a mixing glass with ice. Stir to chill and dilute. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Express the oil from the twist over the drink and discard.

name: Negroni
instructions: Pour all ingredients into an ice-filled old-fashioned glass, and stir approximately 20 seconds. Add garnish.

name: Blood & Sand
instructions: Pour all ingredients into cocktail shaker filled with ice cubes. Shake well. Strain into coupe glass. Flame orange zest over the top of the glass.

name: Harms' Way
instructions: Pour all liquid ingredients into a mixing glass and stir about 55 times for proper dilution and optimal clarity. Strain into a chilled coupe. Twist fresh lemon peel over cocktail for aroma. Garnish with a simple lemon peel, trimmed neatly.

name: Amaretto Rose
instructions: Pour Amaretto and lime juice over ice in a collins glass. Fill with club soda and serve.

name: The Touch  of Glass
instructions: Pour brandy into a brandy snifter. Top with 2-3 drops of truffle tincture. Spray Pipe Tobacco Essence on wrist. Smell wrist, sip drink, and enjoy.

name: La Capilla
instructions:
Pour ingredients in a shaker tin, add ice and shake well. 

Fine strain the drink into a double rocks glass filled with ice.
 
Balance a small watermelon triangle on top of the glass. Grate fresh lime zest on top using a microplane, and then sprinkle with Maldon salt.

name: Palais Royal
instructions: Pour ingredients into a wine glass half-filled with crushed ice. Garnish with a half-slice of lemon and a maraschino cherry.

name: Rothes Julep
instructions: Pour ingredients into cocktail shaker and shake vigorously with ice. Strain into a rocks glass over crushed ice with a mint sprig garnish.

name: Rio De Janeiro Mule
instructions: Pour ingredients into mug, fill with ice. top with ginger ale. Roll into tin. Roll back into mug. Garnish.

name: Fernet Frappe
instructions: Pour ingredients over crushed ice into a stemmed glass, stir well and serve.

name: Red Bud Trail
instructions: Pour into shaker over ice and shake well. Serve straight up in a martini glass with an orange twist.

name: The Twinger
instructions: Pour King's Ginger into an ice-filled collins glass. Top off with Fever-Tree Ginger Ale. Add bitters, and squeeze lime over and serve.

name: No.3 & T
instructions: Pour No.3 gin into a highball glass over ice cubes. Add a slice of lemon and stir. Top up with tonic water to taste.

name: 3 & Tree
instructions: Pour No.3 into ice-filled collins glass. Top off with Fever-Tree Tonic Water to just below rim of the glass. Float .25 oz. of Maurin Quina on top of cocktail. Garnish with small spoonful of fresh pomegranate seeds.

name: Crooked Kilt
instructions: Pour over crushed ice with a mint garnish.

name: Orange Crush
instructions: Pour over crushed ice with zest. Garnish with an orange peel.

name: San Fran Soda
instructions: Pour over ice in a pint glass, then fill with steam beer. Garnish with a lemon wedge and a straw.

name: Simply Perfecto
instructions: Pour Perfecto Amor over ice and garnish with an orange wheel.

name: Château Montifaud Creation
instructions: Pour the Cognac and lemon juice into the glass and then add orgeat syrup; fill with ices cubes and mix with a spoon. At the end, add lemonade and lemon peel.

name: Château Montifaud on the Rocks
instructions: Pour the Cognac into the glass and add ice cubes. Perceive the aromas that are released as a summer rain that reveals the secrets of the nature...

name: Château Montifaud Pink Love
instructions: Pour the Cognac, sugar syrup, lemon juice and 3 pressed raspberries into the shaker, then shake. Strain into the glass (using a cocktail strainer) and fill with chilled Champagne. Garnish with 3 raspberries.

name: Duke's Classic Dry Martini
instructions: Pour the Extra Dry Vermouth into a frozen martini glass (either 7 oz. or 5.5 oz. glass) and coat in a circular motion. Top up the glass with ice cold No.3 gin. Pare the rind of an Amalfi lemon, and give it a twist to extract the oils into the glass.

name: Glimpse of Seduction
instructions: Pour the first four ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth. Serve into a champagne saucer. Top with Pomegranate Liqueur float.

name: Bloody Fernet
instructions: Pour the ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth. Serve in a cocktail glass or coupe.

name: Kentucky Likker
instructions: Pour the ingredients in an old fashioned glass, then fill up with ice and stir well before serving.

name: Château Montifaud Lady Touch Triple Sec
instructions: Pour the ingredients in the shaker. Close and shake well until frosted. Strain into the glass. Garnish with fresh mint.

name: Pink Colada
instructions: Pour the ingredients into a cocktail shaker filled with ice. Shake well. Strain into a chilled cocktail or Collins glass.

name: Profundo
instructions: Pour the liquor ingredients over ice and top with half inch (about one centimeter) layer of lightly whipped cream shaken with Hum Liqueur.

name: Château Montifaud on Mint
instructions: Pour the sugar syrup into the glass and add fresh mint and green lemon. Lightly crush and add ice cubes, Cognac, lemon juice and sparkling water. Mix with a spoon and serve with a straw.

name: Tequila Sunflare
instructions: Pre-freeze pomegranate juice into one large cube. Rinse a glass with Montanaro vermouth. Dump any excess out. Build the remainder of spirits and bitters into a shaker tin. Shake vigorously. Strain over the pomegranate ice cube into a rocks glass.

name: Kiss Of Persuasion
instructions: Pre-mix the liquors together and keep refrigerated. Serve 2 oz. neat into a small coupe glass. Float the fresh cream on top.

name: Marchesa
instructions: Prepare directly in an old fashioned glass.

name: Brazilian Mule Punch
instructions:
Punch Preparation Instructions:
Combine Ypioca, lime and syrup in bowl. Top with ginger beer and Angostura bitters* (amounts will depend on size of punch and number of anticipated servings). Garnish with thin-sliced dried ancho chiles and lime wheels to float. 

*You can optionally skip adding Angostura in punch and instead add a dash or 2 to each individual cocktail as served.


Individual Cocktail Preparation Instructions:
Combine ingredients exactly the same way as a traditional Moscow Mule over crushed ice. Garnish with dash or 2 bitters and ancho chile slices. Mint is an optional garnish.

name: Coco Batida
instructions: Put 1 1/2 ounces of coconut milk, 1 1/2 ounces of condensed milk, 3 ounces of Ypiòca and tablespoon of sugar in a blender. Mix for a few seconds. Add ice cubes and serve.

name: Orange Batida
instructions: Put 1 1/2 ounces, 2 1/2 ounces orange juice, and 1-2 tablespoons of sugar in a shaker. Add ice cubes and shake for a few seconds. Put in glass and serve.

name: Passion Fruit Batida
instructions: Put 1/2 passion fruit inner part in a blender, 1-2 tablespoons of sugar and 50ml ( 1 1/2 ounces) of Ypiòca. Mix for a few seconds. Filter in a glass and serve with ice.

name: Peach Batida
instructions: Put 3 ounces of peach juice, 1 1/2 ounces of Ypiòca, 1 1/2 ounces of condensed milk and 1 tablespoon of sugar in a shaker. Add ice cubes, shake well and serve.

name: Karlsson's Old Fashioned
instructions:
Put a brown sugar cube at the bottom of a frosted glass.
Add a splash of bitter and muddle the sugar.
Add Karlsson's Gold Vodka and stir with ice.
Add lemon and orange peels (which you can burn for added flavor).

name: Gold Rush
instructions: Put basil leaves in mixing glass. Gently muddle the leaves. Add all ingredients except the bitters or ice. Dry shake the cocktail vigorously. Add the ice and shake again vigorously. Double strain into a coupe. Add a few drops of angostura on top, add cracked pepper, slap one single basil leaf on top for garnish.

name: The Improved Sazerac
instructions: Put ingredients in mixing glass. Stir. Strain into glass that has absinthe rinse. Add lemon zest.

name: Luxardo Fresco
instructions: Quick stir and garnish with rosemary.

name: Stranger in the Alps
instructions: Rinse a rocks glass with eau de vie. Combine remaining ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake, and strain over fresh ice into a rocks glass. Garnish with a lemon pigtail.

name: Corpse Reviver No.2
instructions: Rinse cocktail glass with Vieux Pontarlier Absinthe. Shake rest with ice and strain.

name: Sazerac
instructions:
Rinse entire glass with absinthe, discard, if you must.
Stir with ice and strain into an old-fashion glass, garnish with lemon peel.

name: The Last Marasca
instructions: Rinse glass with absinthe. Add a Luxardo Cherry (covered in its juice) to a mixing glass with remaining ingredients. Stir until cold and slightly rose colored. Single strain, serve with cherry from mixing glass.

name: Seersucker Sunshine
instructions: Rinse the glass with Orange Blossom Water and pour cocktail. Shake Egg White, Sauternes and Lemon Juice vigorously dry for 25 seconds, then add ice, gin, Luxardo Bitter Bianco and Lillet Blanc, shake again for 10 seconds. Strain into Nick & Nora, garnish with a lemon wheel.

name: Bubbleliciously Delicious
instructions: Serve cold and garnish with a scoop of vanilla bean ice cream swirled with Luxardo Maraschino Cherry Juice.

name: Wooster 53
instructions:
Serve in a champagne flute up to 60% full and top with tonic water up to 80% full, garnish with a grapefruit twist.

*Ginger/Cardamom/Honey Syrup:
Bring about 8 cups of water to a boil, once boiling turn off heat and add 2 cups of cardamom pods. Let sit for about 6 hours. Strain the pods out, add 1 qt (32 oz) of honey and stir until dissolved. For an added cardamom boost: place the cardamom pods with the cardamom/honey "tea" in a blender. Blend and press through a fine strainer so as to discard the solids but keep the flavor.

name: The King's Mulled Cider

*Serrano Pepper Syrup:
Take 6 serrano peppers. Once charred place in a blender with about 2 cups of water and throw in the 2 remaining fresh serranos (seeds and all). Blend and strain out the solids. Add 1 cup of demarara sugar to the serrano pepper water and stir until it is dissolved.

*Strawberry & Black Pepper Shrub:
-- 1 cup Sliced Strawberries
-- 1 cup Granulated Sugar
-- 1 cup Champagne Vinegar
-- 1 handful of Black Balinese Peppers

Combine all ingredients in a medium bowl. Using a potato masher or a wooden spoon, mash the berries so they release their juices. Stir until the sugar has mostly dissolved and refrigerate overnight.
The next day, remove the mixture to a saucepan and heat gently over medium-low heat. Do not bring to a simmer--you simply want to heat the mixture to coax the sugar to dissolve.
Strain the shrub through a fine mesh strainer. If desired, strain again through a coffee filter. Refrigerate.

*Coconut Pistachio Puree:
-- .5 lb Pistachios (toasted)
-- 1.5 lbs Sugar (144 oz.)
-- 11 oz Coconut Milk
-- 13 oz Water
-- 1 tsp Salt

Toast the pistachios at 250°F for 20 mins. Remove and put in a blender with sugar until well chopped. Combine all the ingredients in a pot and bring to a boil. Let simmer for 20 mins and then remove from the heat. Let cool and place in a large container. Let the mixture sit overnight then strain out the solids. Save the solids to spin in the ice cream maker (if you have one). Spin the solids and add the mixture back into the puree via chinois. Bottle, label and date the syrup (it will keep indefinitely if you freeze it).

*Blood Orange Vanilla Mint Puree:
-- 1 container Boiron Blood Orange Puree (34 oz.)
-- 39 grams Mint (stems and leaves)
-- 1 tsp Vanilla Extract
-- 30 oz White Sugar

Combine all the ingredients except the mint in a pot. 
Bring to a light simmer, stirring to incorporate everything.
Then add the mint and cook for 5 minutes, then remove the pot from the heat and strain through a chinois to remove the mint.
Label and date a container, then place in your fridge. Use within a week.

Grind the chapuline  (dried crickets) and dried chile pepper in a mortar and pestle, as it becomes a powder, throw in the salt and pour onto a plate. Use a fresh lime wedge to moisten the rim of the glass and dip into the powder.

*Toasted Coconut Infused Old Potrero
-- 750ml Old Potrero Straight Rye Whiskey
-- 2.5 oz Toasted Coconut

Combine all ingredients in a clean container and let sit for 8 hours.
Strain through cheesecloth and then funnel back into your Old Potrero bottles.
Keeps indefinitely.

**Turmeric Coconut Cream:
-- 12 g Fresh Turmeric
-- 1 c Water
-- 1 c Honey
-- 3 tsp Black Pepper
-- 12 oz Coconut Milk

Grate fresh turmeric into 1 cup water.
Bring to a boil, add 1 cup of honey and stir.
Remove from heat and let the mix sit for 1:30 hours.
Strain out turmeric and add 3 tsp. ground black pepper.
Add 12 oz. coconut milk and stir.
Place in an airtight container and keep in the fridge. Use within a week.

*Beet Thyme Reduction
-- 520 grams raw beets 
-- 344 grams honey
-- 2 oranges peeled (just the peels)
-- 2 sprigs of thyme
-- pinch of salt

Juice beets, add honey and let dissolve and cook for 15 mins. 
Add thyme and simmer for 5 more mins. 
Strain through a chinois into a container and then place in the fridge.
This makes 1 quart, so multiply as needed.
Use within 1 week.

*Pomegranate Cordial:
-- 48 oz Pomegranate Juice
-- 2.5 lbs White Sugar
-- 1 lb Turbinado Sugar
-- 8 Course Cracked Black Peppercorns
-- 2 Cloves
-- 5 Allspice Berries
-- 1 Tbsp Pomegranate Molasses
-- 1 oz Orange Flower Water

name: The Cavalier White Lady

name: Land of Valencia

To garnish, peel a blood orange skin and express the oils over the cocktail. Grab mint sprigs and slap over the top of hand to express oils, wrap the peel at the base of the mint sprigs, tie off with twine and set on top of large format cube.

*Clarified Coconut Sugar Blood Orange Cordial:
-- Blood Oranges
-- Agar Agar
-- Coconut Sugar

Juice blood oranges. Bring 1/4 of juice to a boil with agar agar. Pour cooked juice into the remaining 3/4 juice. Freeze the juice. Pull the block from the freezer and let drip through cheese cloth and perforated pan creating the clarified juice. Bring the juice together with a rich (2:1) coconut sugar creating a coconut sugar blood orange cordial.

name: Smashing Great Pumpkins

name: Green and Gold

Step one: Infuse the vodka
- Transfer ingredients including 750 ml of Karlsson's vodka to a canning jar or glass container. Close the container.
- Shake and rotate every few hours. Let infuse for 24 hours total.
- After 24 hours, strain the vodka through a cheesecloth or nutmilk bag.
- Transfer the vodka to a glass bottle. Store in the refrigerator or freezer until use.

Step two: Make the martini
- Stir 2-3 oz, depending on preference, of the infused vodka with ice.
- Strain into a chilled martini glass.
- Add pickled onions and peppers.

instructions:
Stir ingredients over cracked ice. With a julep strainer, pour into a chilled cocktail glass. Once poured, top with lemon foam.

*Dry Vermouth Infusion:
-- 1.5 oz Togarashi Spice
-- 3 Vanilla Beans
-- 2 bags of Green Tea
Place ingredients in cheesecloth to steep in a bell jar with 1 bottle of Dolin Dry vermouth for 2 days. 
Remove the infusion elements and refrigerate the infusion.

**Lemon Foam:
-- 1 bsp Lemon Juice
-- 1 bsp Simple Syrup
-- 1 Egg White 
Place ingredients into a shaker tin and whip with a bar whisk into a foam.

*Barley Tea-Infused Nikka Coffey Grain Whiskey:
-- 10 g. Barley Tea (1 package)
-- 750 mls. Nikka Coffey Grain Whisky
In a cambro, empty a bottle of Nikka Coffey Grain Whiskey, add 1 package of barley tea.
Steep for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Strain and store.

name: Karlsson's Winter Punch
instructions: Toast spices and set aside. Bring cranberry juice to a simmer. Tie the spices and orange peel in cheesecloth and add them to the cranberry juice. Let this simmer for about 30 minutes. Remove the spice sachet and add the other liquid ingredients. Serve warm or chilled with orange wheels as a garnish. (The magic of punches is that you can adjust them to your liking as you make them, so please play around with different juices and proportions.)

name: Soleo
instructions: Use an empty bottle of 750ml capacity and combine together 12 ounces - 360 ml of each of the two liqueurs and store in the freezer. Serve very cold in a small cordial glass without ice.

name: Vieja Moda
instructions:
Using a grater, slice 1 peel of grapefruit into a double old-fashioned glass, angling into the glass in order to get the expressed citrus. Then add Cinnamon-Clove Syrup and 3 large ice cubes.

Separately, in a large mixing glass full of ice, pour in Chinaco Anejo Tequila. Using a barspoon, stir for 28-33 rotations. Strain tequila into prepared glass. Add 4 drops of Bittermens Hellfire Bitters. Garnish and enjoy.

*Cinnamon-Clove Syrup:  
-- 2 C Water
-- 1 C Demarea Sugar
-- 4-5 Cinnamon Sticks
-- 7-9 Cloves

Combine Water and Demarea in a pot and bring to a simmer until the sugar is dissolved. Remove from heat. Add Cinnamon Sticks and Cloves and let steep. Leave at room temperature, overnight. Once cooled, strain into a squeeze bottle and refrigerate.

name: Flambeed Brandy Alexander
instructions:
Warm and flame H by Hine VSOP Fine Champagne in metal mug like a Blue Blazer without the tossing back and forth.
Douse flame of heated brandy with cacao, top with foamed cream.
Garnish with freshly grated nutmeg.

name: PB&K Old Fashioned
instructions: Wet half of the rim of a 13 oz. rocks glass with Benedictine. Shake off the excess Benedictine to avoid clumping on the rim and dip it into the peanut butter powder. Flick the glass to shake the excess powder off. Place the glass in a cooler to chill the glass. In a frozen mixing glass combine all liquid ingredients with equal parts cracked ice and whole hard ice. Stir the cocktail for 45 seconds. Remove the glass from the cooler and place a 2 in. hard ice cube in it. Double strain the cocktail into the glass. Spritz the cocktail with both of the twists. Make sure to release all the essential oils into the cocktail. Discard the twists.

name: Fiery Clydesdale
instructions: Wet shake all ingredients except grapefruit soda, pour over ice and top with grapefruit soda. Rim glass with grapefruit demerara. Add dehydrated grapefruit wheel and slice of fresh root ginger for garnish.
